steps.title: Identify the antecedent for the pronoun
steps.content: Before the colon, “dark fiction” is established as a singular subject. After the colon, a new clause elaborates on what dark fiction does, beginning with a subject pronoun that must refer back to “dark fiction.”

steps.title: Apply pronoun-antecedent agreement
steps.content:
The rule is pronoun-antecedent agreement: a pronoun must match its antecedent in number. “It” is singular and correctly refers to “dark fiction,” allowing the sentence to express that dark fiction both entertains readers and uses horror tropes.

The other choices violate this rule or don't fit the context: “they” is plural (conflicting with singular “dark fiction”), “we” is the wrong person, and “one” shifts awkwardly from the specific subject.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
